title: Supporting NEW BUSINESSES Act
plain_summary: This bill creates a program to reward state and local governments that make it easier for people to start small businesses. Every year, the Small Business Administration will give out three awards to different sized governments that do things like reduce paperwork or create helpful websites for new business owners. The goal is to encourage governments to make starting a business simpler and faster for everyone.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Supporting New Entrepreneurs and Workers By Undoing and Streamlining Inhibitors to Nascent Enterprises through Supporting Successful Efficiency Solutions Act. It can also be called the Supporting NEW BUSINESSES Act for short.

sections.title: Section 2 Awards for Streamlining the Process of Business Formation
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Small Business Act. It moves one section to a new number and adds a completely new section. The new section requires the head of the Small Business Administration to give out three awards every year. These awards go to state and local governments that find new and effective ways to make it easier for people to start small businesses in their communities. One award goes to a government that serves at least four hundred thousand people. One award goes to a government that serves at least one hundred thousand people but fewer than four hundred thousand people. One award goes to a government that serves fewer than one hundred thousand people. State and local governments that want these awards must send in applications. The application must be sent at the time, contain the information, and be in the format that the Small Business Administration requires. When deciding who gets the awards, the Small Business Administration will look at whether the government made starting a business easier by reducing paperwork and forms, creating easy to use websites that bring together all the resources people need, reducing duplicate requirements between different government offices, or finding other creative ways to make the process faster and more efficient.
